acceptance_targets: [CH14_AWARD_01] In the revised material around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:63 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:101, at least one dark-comic beat lasts for more than a single line and changes the interaction on the page through embarrassment, social pressure, or transactional bluntness; the chapter should still retain the collapse damage and estrangement, but a reader should feel sustained dark humor before Kem's final southward choice rather than only two isolated wry moments.

[CH14_CRAFT_01] After revision, a reader can point to on-page information in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:17 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:125 that explains how Kem knows Vel's arithmetic. Fail if Kem-limited narration still both withholds perception of Vel's spend and asserts an exact unseen spend or exact unseen total.
[R05] Ch14's self-description does not paraphrase the preservation-critique already delivered in Ch13 L3264.
[R17] Ch14 contains no more than 6 instances of the word 'dust/dusty', with varied vocabulary for the remaining atmospheric references.
[C03] The signet is described with consistent material (silver-dark) in all chapters including Ch14.

[LW06-002] A careful reader can move from Vel at eighteen in chapter 13 through every explicit chapter-14 spend and land on the exact number stated in the camp scene without supplying an off-page deduction.

[LW06-003] Reading chapter 13 straight into chapter 14, the new chapter announces a distinct rhythm within its first page instead of feeling like another variation on 'collapsing Margins structure plus tactical spend.'

[LW06-004] After reading chapters 12 through 14 in sequence, a reader can state a distinct new terminal state created by chapter 14 that is not reducible to 'they keep walking south with fewer levels.'
[LW07-002] The chapter 14 signet beat gives the reader new emotional or social information without re-explaining that House Aldraste treats untouched preservation as a life philosophy.

[LW07-004] Only one chapter in this window delivers the clear 'Kem chooses to keep going south' payoff; the other advances trust damage, silence, or tactical cooperation without duplicating that narrative function.
[length_chapter_14_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 5000 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised material around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:63 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:101, at least one dark-comic beat lasts for more than a single line and changes the interaction on the page through embarrassment, social pressure, or transactional bluntness; the chapter should still retain the collapse damage and estrangement, but a reader should feel sustained dark humor before Kem's final southward choice rather than only two isolated wry moments.
findings.problem: The chapter hits its plot obligations but undercommits the mandated dark-comic mode. The quarryman beat and the signet-child exchange are isolated flashes of humor inside a chapter whose dominant register stays solemn rescue and earnest aftermath, so the back-half tonal contract reads as garnish rather than governing pressure. That costs distinctiveness and keeps the chapter below shortlist-level conviction.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the aftermath and camp passages around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:63; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:73; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:101; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:113 so the settlement's blunt practicality or the signet's social absurdity generates a sustained darkly funny pressure beat, not a one-line garnish. Keep the physical cost and broken trust active in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:79; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:91; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:123; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:147, but let humor keep shaping the scene instead of disappearing once the rescue is over.

findings.acceptance_test: After revision, a reader can point to on-page information in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:17 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:125 that explains how Kem knows Vel's arithmetic. Fail if Kem-limited narration still both withholds perception of Vel's spend and asserts an exact unseen spend or exact unseen total.
findings.problem: The chapter breaks its Kem-limited arithmetic logic. At line 17 the narration states Vel spent exactly one level and immediately says Kem cannot perceive the spend; at line 125 it gives Vel an exact remaining total of sixteen without establishing how he knows it. In a book where Ledger numbers are a core causal system and close-third access is part of the style contract, this reads as authorial convenience rather than earned on-page knowledge.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:17 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:125 so every exact number attached to Vel is either sourced on the page or downgraded to Kem's inference. If Vel is meant to be at sixteen by the camp scene, show the additional spend in a way Kem can track, have Vel state the number, or recast the narration as an estimate instead of an exact total.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch14's self-description does not paraphrase the preservation-critique already delivered in Ch13 L3264.
findings.evidence: Ch13 L3264: 'House Aldraste. Preservation by standing still while everyone else paid.'; Ch14 L3391: 'Means I come from people who think keeping everything untouched counts as a life.'
findings.problem: Kem articulates the same thematic restatement of Aldraste identity -- preservation-as-inaction -- in nearly identical register across the Ch13/Ch14 boundary (127 lines apart). Ch13 is interior monologue, Ch14 is dialogue, but the semantic content ('standing still while everyone else paid' vs 'keeping everything untouched counts as a life') is the same insight.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise Ch14 L3391 so Kem's self-description to the girl carries a different facet of his background -- specificity about what the Vault was like, or what he left behind, rather than the preservation-as-failure formula already stated in Ch13.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch14 contains no more than 6 instances of the word 'dust/dusty', with varied vocabulary for the remaining atmospheric references.
findings.evidence: The word 'dust' (including dusty) appears 13 times in Ch14 (~148 prose lines, ~1,984 prose words). This is 1 occurrence per 11 lines. The collapsed-building rescue context justifies the image, but the word itself becomes mechanical. Manuscript-wide total is 61.
findings.problem: Ch14 has the highest single-chapter dust density: 13 occurrences in a short chapter. The structural collapse naturally produces dust, but repeating the same word 13 times in ~1,984 words makes the prose feel mechanical rather than immersive.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace 6-8 of the weaker 'dust' instances with varied descriptors (grit, powder, haze, particulate, debris cloud) or remove them where the image is already established and the sentence does not need it.

findings.acceptance_test: The signet is described with consistent material (silver-dark) in all chapters including Ch14.
findings.evidence: Ch2 L324: 'silver-dark, heavy'; Ch7 L1537: 'silver, heavy'; Ch13 L3264: 'silver-dark'; Ch14 L3389: 'Gold flashed warm in her palm'; Ch14 L3399: 'Heat from her hand lingered in the gold'; Ch19 L4608: 'Dark metal.'
findings.problem: The Aldraste signet is consistently described as silver/silver-dark from Ch2 through Ch13. At Ch14 L3389 and L3399, it is suddenly described as 'gold.' This is a material inconsistency -- the ring cannot be both silver-dark and gold.
findings.rewrite_direction:
Change Ch14 L3389 from 'Gold flashed warm in her palm' to 'Silver flashed warm in her palm' or 'Metal flashed warm in her palm.' Change L3399 from 'Heat from her hand lingered in the gold' to 'Heat from her hand lingered in the silver' or 'in the metal.'

Aggregator guidance:
- Canonical choice [signet_material]: silver or silver-dark. Grounding: First establishment in Ch2 as silver/silver-dark; consistently maintained through Ch7, Ch13 ('silver-dark, the closed circle crest worn smooth'). Ch14's 'gold' is an isolated contradiction against six prior consistent references.

findings.acceptance_test: A careful reader can move from Vel at eighteen in chapter 13 through every explicit chapter-14 spend and land on the exact number stated in the camp scene without supplying an off-page deduction.
findings.cross_chapter_context: Vel ends Ch13 at 18 levels per the continuity sheet. Ch14 dramatizes one on-page spend (the Perception scan at the collapsed house), then later states Vel has 16 left. The arithmetic is short by one level. Either add a second visible spend on-page — camp setup, route checking, or settlement stabilization — before the evening count appears, or adjust the later total to 17.
findings.evidence: Chapter 14 shows one explicit spend from Vel during the rescue, taking her below the prior count once (snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3301). The evening scene then treats her as being at sixteen levels without naming a second deduction anywhere in between (snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3409).

evidence.excerpt: 3301: No discussion. Vel went to the west side and laid her palm on the stone. The stillness of her body changed in a way Kem knew too well, attention narrowing until she seemed built around it. One level left her there. He could not see the spend, but he saw the answer land in the set of her shoulders.

3409: Instead he watched Vel mark a route through broken ground with sixteen levels left and a hand that had not entirely stopped shaking since the house wall. He tried, for a second, to picture himself taking the north road at dawn. The picture would not hold. It kept breaking on the house, the joist under his palm, the girl's hands on her mother's face.

findings.problem: Chapter 13 hands Vel off at eighteen. Chapter 14 only dramatizes one on-page subtraction before stating she has sixteen left. In a novel that asks the reader to track level arithmetic closely, that invisible missing level reads as a continuity leak rather than background noise.
findings.rewrite_direction: Either change the later count to seventeen or add the missing on-page spend close to where it happens. If the extra level goes into camp setup, route checking, or settlement stabilization, the text needs to say so before the evening count appears.

findings.acceptance_test: Reading chapter 13 straight into chapter 14, the new chapter announces a distinct rhythm within its first page instead of feeling like another variation on 'collapsing Margins structure plus tactical spend.'
findings.evidence: Chapter 13 opens with failing road, a stranger in immediate danger, and Kem burning scarce levels to stabilize infrastructure before anyone can move on (snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3132, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3140). Chapter 14 opens with a collapsing house, Vel calling clipped instructions, and Kem again spending scarce levels in precise increments to keep failing structure from killing strangers (snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3295, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3303, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3341).

evidence.excerpt: 3132: The road failed under a farmer before noon.
3140: Kem threw the Stabilization Charm before he'd finished choosing it.

3295: Then the help call came, high and steady, stripped of panic by repetition. Both of them turned toward the same two-story house at the eastern lip, its upper floor already slanting toward the crack.

3303: "Northwest corner's taking weight," she said. The old operational register had survived the fracture intact. "Southeast is gone. Beam runs north to south and it's cracked midway. Reinforce that. Clear the east door. Woman inside, maybe two children."
3341: One more level. The joist locked hard enough to take weight. The hanging boards settled. Twenty-five.

findings.problem: Two consecutive Kem chapters arrive with almost the same opening machine: Margins infrastructure fails, Vel reads the geometry, Kem spends low numbers to rescue other people, and the scene's main pressure is the cost ledger. After the betrayal reveal at the center of chapter 13, chapter 14 needed a visibly different chapter shape; instead it feels like another pass through an already-familiar emergency pattern.
findings.rewrite_direction: Give chapter 14 a different entry beat before the next rescue, or make the first scene about negotiating the damaged alliance in logistical terms rather than dropping directly into another structural-collapse intervention. The point is not to remove competence but to stop repeating the same chapter-opening engine.

findings.acceptance_test: After reading chapters 12 through 14 in sequence, a reader can state a distinct new terminal state created by chapter 14 that is not reducible to 'they keep walking south with fewer levels.'
findings.evidence: Chapter 12 closes on Vel carrying the pressure of Kem at fifty while the group still heads south (snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3117, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3127). Chapter 13 ends after the betrayal reveal with Kem unable to leave and simply continuing onward (snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3262, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3282). Chapter 14 then stages another north-versus-south debate and resolves it with the same outcome: pack buckled, road unchanged, still heading south (snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3407, snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3431).

evidence.excerpt: 3117: They walked. Behind them Kem was a dark shape against the road. Fifty levels. The number sat in Vel like a cracked tooth.
3127: She walked south, and every mile pressed harder.

3262: He wanted to leave. The impulse came whole and immediate: north, away, let her solve her own crossing. Thirty-one levels said otherwise. North meant broken road, no journal, no map, Sable somewhere behind them. South meant Vel at eighteen, Dren with one bad arm, and the only route any of them had. Leaving was a plan for people who knew where they were going.
3282: He kept walking.

3407: North still existed. The quarryman had offered them solid ground, maybe work in the morning. Twenty-five levels spent sparingly could buy Kem years. He could turn around, disappear into some Spent town, learn to mend roofs or unload carts, spend one point a year and call it wisdom.
3431: They would walk south.

findings.problem: Across three chapter endings, the terminal state keeps cashing out as 'lower numbers, damaged trust, still going south.' Costs intensify, but leverage does not. By the end of chapter 14 the cluster has emotionally rich material, yet it has not converted that material into a materially new condition for the read.
findings.rewrite_direction: Let chapter 14 end on a changed condition rather than another quiet recommitment. That could be explicit terms between Kem and Vel, a concrete new external threat, a lost route option, or some other irreversible shift that makes the next chapter feel structurally different from the previous two.

findings.acceptance_test: The chapter 14 signet beat gives the reader new emotional or social information without re-explaining that House Aldraste treats untouched preservation as a life philosophy.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3264; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3391; Chapter 13 already defines the signet as House Aldraste's preservation logic and dramatizes Kem pulling it off and pocketing it (snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3264; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3270). Chapter 14 then has him explain the same idea again to the settlement child; almost as a paraphrase of the prior beat (snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3391; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3395).

evidence.excerpt: 3264: Kem looked down at his right hand instead. The signet sat on his ring finger, silver-dark, the closed circle crest worn smooth by weeks of sweat and dust. House Aldraste. Preservation by standing still while everyone else paid.
3391: "Family ring," Kem said. "From north. Means I come from people who think keeping everything untouched counts as a life."
3270: Instead he closed his hand and put it in his pocket.
3395: A laugh escaped him before he could decide whether he still knew how. "Yeah," he said. "It was."

findings.problem: Chapter 13 already converts Kem's signet into a clear symbol of Hoarded preservation in his interior narration. The adjacent chapter then has him explain nearly the same idea out loud to the settlement child, so the reader gets the thesis of the ring twice in quick succession.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep the child interaction in Chapter 14, but let Kem answer more evasively or visually so the scene plays as shame, humor, or class exposure rather than a second explanation of what the ring symbolizes. Chapter 13's interior narration already converted the signet into a clear emblem of Hoarded preservation. Do not re-explain that House Aldraste treats untouched preservation as a life philosophy — the reader received that thesis less than a chapter ago. The new scene should add social information (how the Margins recode the crest) or emotional information (why Kem still carries it) without restating thematic meaning.

findings.acceptance_test: Only one chapter in this window delivers the clear 'Kem chooses to keep going south' payoff; the other advances trust damage, silence, or tactical cooperation without duplicating that narrative function.
findings.cross_chapter_context: Ch16 is the chapter that owns Kem's definitive southward recommitment, driven by Vel's full Maren confession and the demand for truth. Soften Ch14's ending into provisional practical cooperation or the Hoarded-comprehension insight without making it the unambiguous 'I choose to keep going' decision. Let the stop/go question remain alive so Ch16 can reopen and definitively resolve it.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3407; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3413; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3431; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3775; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3883; snapshots/cycle_02/FINAL_NOVEL.md:3905

evidence.excerpt: 3407: North still existed. The quarryman had offered them solid ground, maybe work in the morning. Twenty-five levels spent sparingly could buy Kem years. He could turn around, disappear into some Spent town, learn to mend roofs or unload carts, spend one point a year and call it wisdom.
3413: "Where does her writing stop?"
3431: They would walk south.
3775: "No more steps. Not until you tell me why."
3883: "Okay I'll keep walking." Kem stood. His knees cracked. "Not because it's fine. You used me. Same road, same number, you'd do it again."

3905: At the first stretch of shade worth the name, Vel crouched with it open on a broken culvert wall and showed Kem the last pages: the abandoned settlement west of the basin, the old gallery line that might still offer cover, the descent she thought the Reserve watched least. He asked practical questions now. Distances. Water. How many bad options were still ahead. Vel answered them before he had to ask twice.

findings.problem: The window resolves Kem's decision to continue south twice. Chapter 14 already gives him a full recommitment beat built around the route journal, tomorrow's ground, and a packed bedroll for south. Chapter 16 then reopens the stop/go question, delivers the confession, and ends with another explicit 'I'll keep walking' plus more route-journal logistics. Read consecutively, the second beat has to reopen a decision the earlier chapter already closed.
findings.rewrite_direction: Soften chapter 14's ending into provisional practical cooperation or Hoarded-comprehension without making it the definitive southward recommitment. Let chapter 16 own the single unambiguous decision-to-continue beat after Vel's confession.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 5000 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

Every narration sentence must be grammatically complete. Do not use article-stripped noun-phrase fragments, subjectless stubs, or telegraphic fragment strings as a rhythmic device. Isolated level-count callouts ('Twelve.' or 'Zero.') are acceptable; stacked fragment clusters ('Not night. Underground dark.' or 'Too slow. Too loud. Too late.') are not. Achieve compression through short complete sentences, not by dropping subjects and articles.

When a scene beat has landed through concrete image, action, dialogue, or bodily sensation, do not add an abstract summary sentence that restates its meaning in thesis language. If a paragraph's only job is to translate what the reader already understood from the prior image or line of dialogue, cut it or replace it with a new fact, consequence, or pressure. Trust the scene. One beat, one landing.

No character should deliver a multi-sentence thematic diagnosis, balanced mini-speech, or quotable aphorism during a pressured scene. Accusations, confessions, and revelations must arrive through messy, character-true speech: interruptions, self-corrections, false starts, incomplete grammar, practical redirects, and mid-sentence recalibrations. If a line of dialogue can be lifted as a standalone epigram, it is probably too composed for the scene pressure. Rhetorical questions used for emphasis rather than genuine inquiry are not permitted for Vel.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about a world where doing nothing is rational and doing something is self-destruction. Both the comedy and the darkness must be fully committed. Do not soften combat into choreography or spectacle — fights should hurt, cost real things, and leave damage. Do not soften the comedy into gentle satire — the absurdity of the Hoarded and their agents should be genuinely funny, sometimes cruelly so. Do not soften grief into sentimentality or revelation into epiphany. The premise demands a tonal register that holds absurdity and genuine pain in the same hand. When the material is uncomfortable — the physical reality of injury, the moral weight of exploitation, the specific grief of losing someone to a place you cannot reach — lean into specificity rather than retreating to safety. A draft that never risks anything produces a novel that never moves anyone.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in combat, injury, consequence, or emotional extremity is a craft failure. If Vel breaks ribs, the reader should feel the break — the specific wrongness of breathing with cracked bone, the way she holds herself, the pain when she forgets. If Kem is terrified, the terror should be specific and physical: hands, stomach, breathing, the precise quality of his fear. Do not abstract away from embodied experience into summary or generalization.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Sable (low) and early-book Kem (initially low, shifting to high). Uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, formal Hoarded speech contexts, or bureaucratic register. In casual conversation, unjustified full-form lines should be treated as suspect.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Vary chapter rhythm aggressively. Combat chapters should be compressed and physical. Discovery chapters should have more room for observation and the surprise of new information. Quiet chapters should breathe — longer scenes, more sensory detail, conversations that circle rather than drive. Intercut chapters (like Ch 11's Kem/Sable split and Ch 17's Sable intercut) should have contrasting textures between sections. No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural shape: vary scene count, dialogue density, pacing, and the ratio of action to interiority. Dark comedy chapters in the back half (Ch 12, 14, 15, 17) must commit fully to comic energy as a primary narrative mode: the absurdity of Hoarded bureaucratic language applied to human exploitation, the grim humor of fighting for your life with single-digit levels, the comedy of precision teamwork performed in emotional silence. These chapters carry genuine stakes alongside their comedy — the tonal fusion is the point, not tonal alternation.
prose_style_profile.diction: Concrete, physical, occasionally wry. Avoids both literary pretension and genre cliche. The world has its own vocabulary (Hoarded, Spent, Ledger, Stillness, the Scour, the Margins) and these terms should be used naturally, not defined in exposition. Metaphors earned from the world's own materials — spending, counting, weight, erosion — rather than imported from outside the setting. The Hoarded institutional register — bureaucratic classifications, procedural euphemism, administrative language applied to human exploitation — is a specific comic instrument in the Margins chapters. When rendering Hoarded documents, policies, or institutional speech, the language should be precise, procedural, and devastating in its indifference.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Narrator-as-comedian: comedy arises from situation and character, not from narrative commentary or winking asides to the reader
Ability-use described like video game tooltips: do not produce stat readouts or system notifications in prose
Emotional temperature permanently at maximum: not every scene is a crisis and not every internal moment is an epiphany
Generic fantasy description: landscape, architecture, and weather must be specific to this world's conditions, not recycled from the fantasy-prose commons
Competence porn: do not let characters be consistently impressive — they should fumble, miscalculate, and fail at things that are hard
Thematic underlining: the prose should not highlight its own meanings or signal when something is symbolically significant
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third-person limited, shifting with the POV character. Vel chapters are compressed and physical — the prose should feel like it is counting. Kem chapters are more expansive and curious — the prose has more room for observation and surprise. Sable chapters are structured and institutional, loosening as she changes. The POV break in Ch 13–14 (two consecutive Kem chapters) should make Vel's absence felt: the reader is locked inside Kem's betrayed perspective, and the prose should reflect the claustrophobia of a mind cycling through anger without access to the other person's interiority. The distance should be intimate enough to access characters' internal arithmetic but not so close it becomes stream-of-consciousness.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter texture and scene pressure. Short declarative sentences in combat. Longer, conditional, clause-heavy sentences in reflection. Comma-heavy run-ons in panic or exhilaration. The Ledger's arithmetic should bleed into prose rhythm during ability-use moments — characters counting, weighing, calculating. Quiet chapters should breathe. Action chapters should compress. Two consecutive chapters should not share the same dominant rhythm.
sensory_bias: proprioceptive awareness — the body's internal sense of position, strain, damage, and capacity
physical weight and effort — characters feel gravity, distance, fatigue
the Ledger's feedback — a character's awareness of their remaining levels as a persistent background sensation
environmental specificity — terrain, weather, architecture rendered through what a body encounters
